verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est verbum Domini ad Jonam secundo, dicens :
verses.douay_rheims: And the word of the Lord came to Jonah the second time saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Herran sana tapahtui toisen kerran Jonalle ja sanoi:
verses.king_james: And the word of the Lord came unto Jonah the second time, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Surge, et vade in Niniven, civitatem magnam, et prædica in ea prædicationem quam ego loquor ad te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ק֛וּם לֵ֥ךְ אֶל־נִֽינְוֵ֖ה הָעִ֣יר הַגְּדוֹלָ֑ה וּקְרָ֤א אֵלֶ֙יהָ֙ אֶת־הַקְּרִיאָ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר אָנֹכִ֖י דֹּבֵ֥ר אֵלֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Arise, and go to Ninive, the great city: and preach in it the preaching that I bid thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Nouse, mene suureen kaupunkiin Niniveen, saarnaamaan siellä sitä saarnaa, jonka minä sinulle sanon.
verses.king_james: Arise, go unto Nineveh, that great city, and preach unto it the preaching that I bid thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et surrexit Jonas, et abiit in Niniven juxta verbum Domini : et Ninive erat civitas magna, itinere trium dierum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֣קׇם יוֹנָ֗ה וַיֵּ֛לֶךְ אֶל־נִֽינְוֵ֖ה כִּדְבַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֑ה וְנִֽינְוֵ֗ה הָיְתָ֤ה עִיר־גְּדוֹלָה֙ לֵֽאלֹהִ֔ים מַהֲלַ֖ךְ שְׁלֹ֥שֶׁת יָמִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jonah arose, and went to Ninive, according to the word of the Lord: now Ninive was a great city of three days' journey.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin Jona nousi ja meni Niniveen Herran sanan jälkeen. Ja Ninive oli Jumalan suuri kaupunki, kolmen päiväkunnan matka.
verses.king_james: So Jonah arose, and went unto Nineveh, according to the word of the Lord. Now Nineveh was an exceeding great city of three days’ journey.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cœpit Jonas introire in civitatem itinere diei unius : et clamavit, et dixit : Adhuc quadraginta dies, et Ninive subvertetur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֤חֶל יוֹנָה֙ לָב֣וֹא בָעִ֔יר מַהֲלַ֖ךְ י֣וֹם אֶחָ֑ד וַיִּקְרָא֙ וַיֹּאמַ֔ר ע֚וֹד אַרְבָּעִ֣ים י֔וֹם וְנִֽינְוֵ֖ה נֶהְפָּֽכֶת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Jonah began to enter into the city one day's journey: and he cried and said: Yet forty days and Ninive shall be destroyed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Jona rupesi siinä käymään yhden päiväkunnan matkan, huusi hän ja sanoi: vielä on neljäkymmentä päivää; niin Niniven pitää hukkuman.
verses.king_james: And Jonah began to enter into the city a day’s journey, and he cried, and said, Yet forty days, and Nineveh shall be overthrown.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et crediderunt viri Ninivitæ in Deum, et prædicaverunt jejunium, et vestiti sunt saccis, a majore usque ad minorem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיַּאֲמִ֛ינוּ אַנְשֵׁ֥י נִֽינְוֵ֖ה בֵּֽאלֹהִ֑ים וַיִּקְרְאוּ־צוֹם֙ וַיִּלְבְּשׁ֣וּ שַׂקִּ֔ים מִגְּדוֹלָ֖ם וְעַד־קְטַנָּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the men of Ninive believed in God: and they proclaimed a fast, and put on sackcloth from the greatest to the least.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Niniven miehet uskoivat Jumalan päälle, ja antoivat saarnata, että paastottaisiin, ja pukivat säkit yllensä sekä pienet että suuret.
verses.king_james: So the people of Nineveh believed God, and proclaimed a fast, and put on sackcloth, from the greatest of them even to the least of them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et pervenit verbum ad regem Ninive : et surrexit de solio suo, et abjecit vestimentum suum a se, et indutus est sacco, et sedit in cinere.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּגַּ֤ע הַדָּבָר֙ אֶל־מֶ֣לֶךְ נִֽינְוֵ֔ה וַיָּ֙קׇם֙ מִכִּסְא֔וֹ וַיַּעֲבֵ֥ר אַדַּרְתּ֖וֹ מֵֽעָלָ֑יו וַיְכַ֣ס שַׂ֔ק וַיֵּ֖שֶׁב עַל־הָאֵֽפֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the word came to the king of Ninive: and he rose up out of his throne, and cast away his robe from him, and was clothed in sackcloth, and sat in ashes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Niniven kuningas sen kuuli, nousi hän istuimeltansa ja riisui kalliit vaatteensa, kääri itsensä säkkiin ja istui tuhkaan.
verses.king_james: For word came unto the king of Nineveh, and he arose from his throne, and he laid his robe from him, and covered him with sackcloth, and sat in ashes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et clamavit, et dixit in Ninive ex ore regis et principum ejus, dicens : Homines, et jumenta, et boves, et pecora non gustent quidquam : nec pascantur, et aquam non bibant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּזְעֵ֗ק וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ בְּנִֽינְוֵ֔ה מִטַּ֧עַם הַמֶּ֛לֶךְ וּגְדֹלָ֖יו לֵאמֹ֑ר הָאָדָ֨ם וְהַבְּהֵמָ֜ה הַבָּקָ֣ר וְהַצֹּ֗אן אַֽל־יִטְעֲמוּ֙ מְא֔וּמָה אַ֨ל־יִרְע֔וּ וּמַ֖יִם אַל־יִשְׁתּֽוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he caused it to be proclaimed and published in Ninive, from the mouth of the king and of his princes, saying: Let neither men nor beasts, oxen, nor sheep taste anything: let them not feed, nor drink water.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja antoi kuuluttaa ja sanoa Ninivessä kuninkaan tahdosta ja hänen sankareinsa käskystä, sanoen: ettei yksikään ihminen taikka eläin, karja eli lampaat pidä mitään maistaman eikä laitumelle ajettaman, ja ei vettäkään juoman;
verses.king_james: And he caused it to be proclaimed and published through Nineveh by the decree of the king and his nobles, saying, Let neither man nor beast, herd nor flock, taste any thing: let them not feed, nor drink water:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et operiantur saccis homines et jumenta, et clament ad Dominum in fortitudine : et convertatur vir a via sua mala, et ab iniquitate quæ est in manibus eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיִתְכַּסּ֣וּ שַׂקִּ֗ים הָֽאָדָם֙ וְהַבְּהֵמָ֔ה וְיִקְרְא֥וּ אֶל־אֱלֹהִ֖ים בְּחׇזְקָ֑ה וְיָשֻׁ֗בוּ אִ֚ישׁ מִדַּרְכּ֣וֹ הָֽרָעָ֔ה וּמִן־הֶחָמָ֖ס אֲשֶׁ֥ר בְּכַפֵּיהֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And let men and beasts be covered with sackcloth, and cry to the Lord with all their strength, and let them turn every one from his evil way, and from the iniquity that is in their hands.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan ihmiset ja eläimet pitää säkkiin puetettaman, ja heidän pitää lujasti Jumalan tykö huutaman. Ja jokainen palatkaan pahasta tiestänsä ja kätensä väkivallasta.
verses.king_james: But let man and beast be covered with sackcloth, and cry mightily unto God: yea, let them turn every one from his evil way, and from the violence that is in their hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis scit si convertatur et ignoscat Deus, et revertatur a furore iræ suæ, et non peribimus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִֽי־יוֹדֵ֣עַ יָשׁ֔וּב וְנִחַ֖ם הָאֱלֹהִ֑ים וְשָׁ֛ב מֵחֲר֥וֹן אַפּ֖וֹ וְלֹ֥א נֹאבֵֽד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who can tell if God will turn, and forgive: and will turn away from his fierce anger, and we shall not perish?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kukaties Jumala taitaa kääntyä ja katua, ja lakata hirmuisesta vihastansa, ettemme huku.
verses.king_james: Who can tell if God will turn and repent, and turn away from his fierce anger, that we perish not?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et vidit Deus opera eorum, quia conversi sunt de via sua mala : et misertus est Deus super malitiam quam locutus fuerat ut faceret eis, et non fecit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֤רְא הָֽאֱלֹהִים֙ אֶת־מַ֣עֲשֵׂיהֶ֔ם כִּי־שָׁ֖בוּ מִדַּרְכָּ֣ם הָרָעָ֑ה וַיִּנָּ֣חֶם הָאֱלֹהִ֗ים עַל־הָרָעָ֛ה אֲשֶׁר־דִּבֶּ֥ר לַעֲשׂוֹת־לָהֶ֖ם וְלֹ֥א עָשָֽׂה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And God saw their works, that they were turned from their evil way: and God had mercy with regard to the evil which he had said that he would do to them, and he did it not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin Jumala näki heidän työnsä, että he palasivat pahasta tiestänsä, katui Jumala sitä pahaa, jota hän oli puhunut heille tehdäksensä, ja ei tehnytkään.
verses.king_james: And God saw their works, that they turned from their evil way; and God repented of the evil, that he had said that he would do unto them; and he did it not.
